publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-07-30 18:58:05 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Fliers, newspapers, magazines, correspondence, manuscripts, and photographs.
did.abstract: Collection includes original art, photographs and fliers, a 1977 fan club application, press kits, press releases, files of handwritten lyrics, letters, press clippings and magazines, publicity and business plans, guest lists, fan club mailing lists, and other business records and correspondence

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Screamers were an American punk rock group formed in 1975 that participated in the first wave Los Angeles, California punk scene. Often categorized as electropunk, their sound included a synthesizer (ARP Odyssey) and electric piano (Fender Rhodes). Other punk musicians and bands have cited the Screamers as a key influence. The group never released an official recording of any kind, although several bootleg recordings of rehearsals or live performances have since appeared; they have been called one of the great unrecorded groups in rock history. Artist Gary Panter's logo for the band, a stylized cartoon of a screaming head with spiked hair, became one of the most recognizable images to emerge from punk rock.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes original art, photographs and fliers, a 1977 fan club application, press kits, press releases, files of handwritten lyrics, letters, press clippings and magazines, including TOLO, the Franklin High School (Seattle, WA) Newsletter with articles authored by Tommy Tibbits (Gear), a notebook that belonged to Tomata du Plenty (1978), touring, publicity and business plans, guest lists, fan club mailing lists, plans to release a debut album in video form, and other business records and correspondence

emph.ref: Series III. Lyric sheets and booklets
Series IV. Photographs, original art, and recordings
Series VI. Publicity and press materials

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Screamers collection, #8126. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: The Screamers (Musical group).
Punk rock musicians -- California -- Los Angeles.
Punk culture -- California -- Los Angeles.
Punk rock musicians.
Punk culture.
Punk rock music.
Gender identity

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various contracts, m/s and typed, for The Screamers shows, appearances, tapes, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various documentations of payments for shows, most by hand, on backs of Xeroxed fliers, calendars, etc.

did.unittitle: Correspondence from Tommy Gear to television stations and zines

did.physdesc: Annotated on bottom left corner, "Photo: Melanie Nissen"

Featuring Gary Panter "Screamers image." Annotated on verso, "Tomata – Your friends from Seattle are here. Went down to Cantaberry for a while and will return soon. And your friend from Van Nuys came and went. He was in a poetic and exciting inspirational positive force space sourcing real – Bring Bruce! … Old At the Errol Flynn Estate Bring Hot Dogs!" Xeroxed.

scopecontent.p: Specifies whether it is a photo, essay or interview. Typed

did.unittitle: Various Xeroxed press clippings for packet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20x pg., clippings, photos, press articles, concert reviews

did.physdesc: Original master copy flier, pre-Xerox, cut and paste

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roxy in Los Angeles, 7/ 19- 7/21/79, Gary Panter logo, created on Hustler Humor and Slam Magazine paper stock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 x mini Gary Panter Screamers logo and title, business cards/tickets, 2 uncut, 1 cut, and on Hustler Humor and Slam Magazine paper stock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Screamers booking phone number, back page of press kit, print glued to on uncut magazine stock
